rag.description: Export all Google Tasks lists for the authenticated user during an automation run, using either a direct OAuth token or a stored credentials reference. It enforces required authentication checks, returns a complete lists collection, and stores the result as Base64-encoded JSON in a session parameter so downstream steps can reliably resolve list ids and reuse the same data without repeated API calls.

qa.question: What does the ExportGmailTaskLists plugin do?
qa.answer: ExportGmailTaskLists retrieves all Google Tasks lists for the authenticated user and stores the list collection in a session parameter so later steps can discover and reuse existing task lists.

qa.question: What parameters and key features does ExportGmailTaskLists support?
qa.answer: It supports optional Token and Credentials inputs. Token supplies a direct OAuth access token, while Credentials references a stored credential record to obtain a fresh token at runtime. It exports all lists and stores the result in the ExportGmailTaskLists:Result session parameter.

qa.question: How does ExportGmailTaskLists fit into the integration context?
qa.answer: It runs as an Action in the sequential workflow model (componentType “task”) and uses the Google Tasks API to fetch task lists so other automation steps can work with lists without opening the Google Tasks interface.

qa.question: What are best practices for using ExportGmailTaskLists?
qa.answer: Prefer Credentials over embedding raw tokens, reuse ExportGmailTaskLists:Result instead of repeating the API call, and handle the case where the returned list collection is empty.

qa.question: What rule and schema details should I follow to run ExportGmailTaskLists?
qa.answer: Create an Action rule with pluginName set to ExportGmailTaskLists and pass either {{$--token:value}} or {{$--credentials:value}} via the rule argument. The output is a Base64-encoded JSON value stored in the ExportGmailTaskLists:Result session parameter.

qa.question: What is the manifest key of this plugin?
What manifestVersion is used by this plugin?
What is the pluginType of ExportGmailTaskLists?
Which platforms are supported by this plugin?

qa.answer: The plugin is listed under the “GoogleTasks” category.

qa.question: Who is the author of this plugin manifest?
What author link is provided in the manifest metadata?
What is the summary of ExportGmailTaskLists?
qa.answer: It exports all Google Tasks lists using Token or Credentials and stores the results as Base64-encoded JSON in the ExportGmailTaskLists:Result session parameter for workflow reuse.

qa.question: What source descriptions and Git links are relevant for ExportGmailTaskLists?
qa.answer: The action documentation explains exporting task lists and storing the results for workflow reuse: https://github.com/g4-api/g4-plugins/blob/main/docs/Actions/ExportGmailTasksList.md. The source code shows the API call and response handling implementation: https://github.com/g4-api/g4-plugins/blob/main/src/G4.Plugins.Google/Actions/ExportGmailTasksList.cs.

qa.question: How does ExportGmailTaskLists handle errors and unexpected responses?
qa.answer: It expects a successful Google Tasks API response and requires the response JSON to include an items collection. Workflows should handle invalid or expired tokens, credential resolution failures, insufficient OAuth scopes, network timeouts, malformed responses, non-success status codes, or failures writing ExportGmailTaskLists:Result.

description: ### Purpose
The ExportGmailTaskLists plugin retrieves all Google Tasks lists for the authenticated user.
It helps automation workflows discover existing task lists without opening the Google Tasks interface.
The plugin supports either a direct OAuth access token or a stored credentials reference that generates a fresh token at runtime.
The exported lists are stored as a Base64-encoded JSON value in a session parameter so later workflow steps can safely reuse the data.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature | Description |
| Export Task Lists      | Retrieves all task lists associated with the authenticated Google account.                       |
| Token Authentication   | Accepts a direct OAuth Bearer access token for API access.                                       |
| Credential Resolution  | Uses a stored credential record to generate a fresh access token at runtime.                     |
| Session Result Storage | Saves the returned lists as Base64-encoded JSON in a session parameter for later workflow steps. |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case                 | Description                                                                             |
| Discover Task Lists      | Retrieve all available Google Tasks lists before selecting one for automation.          |
| Dynamic Workflow Routing | Use the exported lists to decide which list should receive new tasks during execution.  |
| Data Synchronization     | Export task lists so automation workflows can synchronize data with external systems.   |
| Monitoring Automation    | Periodically retrieve task lists to verify that expected lists exist in a user account. |

edge_cases: Credentials value is provided and overrides the token value
ExportGmailTaskLists:Result Session parameter cannot be written
Google Tasks API returns malformed JSON
Google Tasks API returns non-success status code
Insufficient OAuth scopes for the Google Tasks API
Invalid or expired OAuth access token
Missing token
Network timeout or connection failure
No task lists exist for the user (items missing or empty array)
Response JSON missing required property: items
Token contains leading/trailing whitespace
Token is empty or whitespace-only
annotations.expected_result: A JSON array of task lists is returned and stored in the ExportGmailTaskLists:Result Session parameter.
annotations.notes: The action uses the OAuth access token value to call the Google Tasks tasklists.list endpoint and expects the response to include an items array of task lists.

description: ### ExportGmailTaskLists: Export using access token
Retrieve all Google Tasks lists for the authenticated user by calling the Google Tasks API with an OAuth access token.
Store the returned JSON array in the ExportGmailTaskLists:Result Session parameter.
A macro token produces a runtime token value that is substituted where the token appears, and the ExportGmailTaskLists action uses that substituted value as the request authorization.

edge_cases: Both credentials and token are provided and the token value is ignored
Credentials id/name not found
Credentials value contains leading/trailing whitespace
Credentials value is empty or whitespace-only
ExportGmailTaskLists:Result Session parameter cannot be written
Google OAuth token endpoint returns non-success status code
Google Tasks API returns malformed JSON
Google Tasks API returns non-success status code
Insufficient OAuth scopes for the Google Tasks API
Network timeout or connection failure
No task lists exist for the user (items missing or empty array)
Refresh-token exchange fails
Response JSON missing required property: items
Stored credential record missing refresh token
annotations.expected_result: A JSON array of task lists is returned and stored in the ExportGmailTaskLists:Result Session parameter.
annotations.notes: The action resolves a stored credential record by id or name, exchanges its refresh token for an access token, and then calls the Google Tasks tasklists.list endpoint expecting an items array in the response.

description: ### ExportGmailTaskLists: Export using credentials reference
Retrieve all Google Tasks lists by resolving a stored credentials reference and exchanging it for an OAuth access token at runtime.
Store the returned JSON array in the ExportGmailTaskLists:Result Session parameter.

A macro token produces a runtime credentials reference value that is substituted where the credentials reference appears, and the ExportGmailTaskLists action uses that substituted value to obtain the access token and authorize the API request.
Stores the exported Google Tasks lists returned by the Google Tasks API.
Contains a Base64-encoded JSON array representing all task lists retrieved during the automation run.
Allows later workflow steps to safely decode and reuse the exported lists without repeating the API request.
outputParameters.name: ExportGmailTaskLists:Result

description: Provides an OAuth Bearer access token used to authenticate requests to the Google Tasks API.
Allows an automation workflow to call the API directly without retrieving credentials from a stored record.
Is ignored when the Credentials parameter is supplied because stored credentials take precedence for generating a token.
parameters.name: Token

description: Specifies the identifier or name of a stored credential record used to obtain an OAuth access token.
Triggers a runtime exchange that generates a fresh access token using GooglePlugin.NewAccessToken.
Provides a reusable and safer authentication approach by avoiding hard-coded access tokens in automation steps.
parameters.name: Credentials

summary: The ExportGmailTaskLists plugin exports all Google Tasks lists for the authenticated user.
It supports either a direct OAuth access token or a stored credentials reference that is exchanged for a fresh token at runtime.
It saves the returned task lists as a Base64-encoded JSON value in a session parameter so later workflow steps can reuse the data safely.
